frameworks.name: international brain laboratory
frameworks.one_line: Multi-lab consortium coordinating standardized decision-making experiments across sites.

frameworks.name: standardized mouse decision-making
frameworks.one_line: Shared mouse behavioural paradigm enabling cross-lab comparability.

frameworks.name: brain-wide neural recording
frameworks.one_line: Coordinated measurements of activity across many mouse brain structures.

core_question: How can a multi-lab consortium dissect the brain-wide neural computations underlying decision-making in mice?
key_assumptions: Decision-making engages coordinated activity across multiple brain structures.
Standardizing behaviour across labs enables meaningful pooling of neural data.
interpretations: A coordinated multi-lab effort combining shared behaviour, brain-wide recordings, and theory can uncover the neural computations of decision-making.
